title: Skill Update Poll and Apply Flow
interaction_flow: SyncEngine → Hub: fetch inbound messages (poll loop)
SyncEngine → EvoMapProxy: onInboundReceived() callback (triggered when received > 0)
SkillUpdater → MailboxStore: poll({ type: 'skill_update' }) → returns msg[]
loop for each msg in updates
processSkillUpdate: guard — if !skillPath → logger.warn, return false
processSkillUpdate: guard — if !content → logger.warn, return false
SkillUpdater → FileSystem: if skill file exists → fs.copyFileSync(skillPath, skillPath+'.bak')
pollAndApply → EvoMapProxy: return applied (count of successful updates)
alternate_flows: processSkillUpdate: on fs/write error → logger.error, return false (no ack, message stays in queue)
SyncEngine: if onInboundReceived callback throws → logger.warn, swallowed (non-fatal)
EvoMapProxy: if skillUpdater.pollAndApply() throws → logger.warn, swallowed
key_relationships: EvoMapProxy instantiates SkillUpdater({ store, skillPath, logger })
EvoMapProxy passes onInboundReceived callback to SyncEngine constructor
SyncEngine invokes onInboundReceived when inbound message count > 0
SkillUpdater depends_on FileSystem (fs, path — Node built-ins)
routes.js receives skillUpdater reference via buildRoutes() extensions param (for direct HTTP trigger path)

data_shapes.skill_update message: { id, type:'skill_update', payload: { content: string, skill_content?: string, version?: string } }

trigger_mechanism: Event-driven: SyncEngine's inbound poll loop fires onInboundReceived callback → synchronous call to pollAndApply() after each batch of inbound messages received from hub
file_side_effects: Creates directory tree if missing (recursive mkdir)
Backs up existing skill file as <skillPath>.bak before overwrite
Writes new skill content to skillPath (utf8)
important_notes: No interval/cron — SkillUpdater does NOT self-poll; it is always triggered by SyncEngine's inbound callback. setSkillPath() allows runtime reconfiguration of the target file. Failed writes do NOT ack the message, so it will be retried on the next inbound cycle.
